title_guess: Hendon RAF Museum & London (Courtauld, Science Museum, Tate Modern, Globe)

pages.text:
Charles did his homework & we wrote & read. The campsite has hot showers, lots of space & wonderful views over fields & dry-stone walls. All for £5 per night.
__Sunday Sept 24th.__
    It was misty & then started to rain. We drove towards Birmingham looking for a church & found St. Oswald's Ashbourne. Bell-ringing. Tonic stayed in the loo in the van. The church is pretty with lots of old Tombs. Cockraine family. Mediaeval & Tudor. At coffee in the church hall the men were friendly but not the women. Perhaps they didn't like the mud on my boots. ABCP service. We set off to reach home for lunch.
__Sat October 20th.__
    We left home at 9.50. & drove to Coleshill & bought a Brompton folding bike. Then we had lunch in the carpark at the Hendon RAF museum. Peter went on a flight simulator. John liked the Radar. Charles liked looking at the model aeroplanes.

(These were made by a museum guard.) I saw the life histories of the Battle-of-Britain pilots. We went on a B of B tour with a very good film about the RAF's role in WW II.

     We arrived at the campsite at 5, put up the tent & saw "Billy Elliot" at 6. Then Thomas arrived & we had a hot dog & ice cream.

__Sun Oct 21st.__
     With mild weather, camping is comfortable. We drove to David's food & left the van & got to St. Margaret's Westminster. A lovely church, a sermon on Hinduism & leaving ambition behind so you're not embittered when you get old. Peter & William joined us.

     Peter had spent the night in London with Mark An at Mark's sister's house. He left a message on Simon's phone, but that was switched off until 12.35. William came down from Cambrid[ge]

We had lunch in a delicatessen & went to the Courtauld at Somerset House. A marvellous museum with old French ivories, 2 men playing cards with each other - Cézanne & the tree over Mt. St. Victoire by the lake. Icons temporary exhib.

     William left, then we went to Covent Garden, where Simon & Charles visited the Transport Museum & John had a caricature done.

     We have arrived at David & Sally's. Dylan has gone to stay with a friend at Northampton. Sally had to stay at home to settle Jem who had a cold. So we took Thomas & David & Maia out to a Turkish meal at Iznik. It was delicious food - felafel. David drove us there & back in his van. Then we drove back to the campsite, but we had to camp in the carpark

because the barrier shut at 10.pm. Peter took a torch to his tent.
__Monday__
     It's raining. It rained intermittently all day. Peter washed up breakfast + we set off on the bus. The bus service here is appalling. 3 large, empty buses go the other way, + when our bus arrived it could only take half the people who were waiting.
     We left at 10 + reached Hamleys at 11. We left John + Charles there for 2 hours (Peter had gone off) + Simon + Eve went to art galleries. We found a beautiful picture of a girl in pink. Eve stopped at Liberty's for Mary Haines' green baize for a card table + Simon helped Charles choose a remote control car. We had lunch on the pavement outside a Pret à Manger. Then we set off for the Science Museum.

captions: In 1949 scientists predicted that in the future computers would weigh no more than 1.5 tons — advance bookings 0870 8704771 enquiries 0870 8704868 Sci√m

pages.text: Simon looked at maths + computers. Charles saw (rockets) the child area + made a bridge using counter weights.

captions: Astronauts cannot cry in space because there is no gravity and tears cannot flow
pages.text:
John looked at motors. Eve looked at George III's science collection – beautiful wooden models. He had the Royal Observatory built in 1769 to watch Venus.
   We had tea + trained + bussed back to the camp. Simon bought tickets for S+E+C for [?Bian] 'Dinosaur' + John went to 'Nutty Professor 2.'
We also had pujas in the badly run restaurant, after which Peter arrived. After the film, we sat in the van + drank beer, waiting for John whose film was later.

Tuesday We reached the __Tate Modern__ at 11.15. + met up with Thomas. It was as expected. The building has high quality benches – inlaid rubber. I liked Brancusi Fish 1926. Bronze fish on a bronze pond. Not fins + scales but the essence of its spirit.

I also like Matisse's snail & Barbara Hepworth's harp. Charles was shocked by the naked dancing man & the woman talking about the bad things that had happened in her relationships (both films). We saw Pat Gregory & Keith Herbert. I would have enjoyed it more as an ice rink.
	 We said goodbye to Thomas & Peter left & we found a good restaurant & had a delicious meal - I had mushroom & stilton & herb crumble (& balsamic [?vinegar]) And for pudding John & I had pineapple & pecan cheesecake with orange syrup sauce. They had used the lighting well.
	 After that we went to the Globe theatre, where we could not stay long enough. A good Scotswoman, Margaret,

captions: The Queen is at this 12 June performance Triumphs & Mirth, 1997, [illegible]
pages.text: gave us a tour. The wood is unseasoned oak. It's the first thatched building in London since the 1666 fire. The marbled column was one of 2 oak trees, with a carved Corinthian capital, one found in Suffolk, one in Scotland. The Queen is at this 12 June performance Triumphs & Mirth, 1997, [illegible]

in honour of Sam Wanamaker's birthday.

        We tried to get back to the campsite for 4.30. But yet again the bus didn't come. So we got in a taxi. But his engine was flat, so he had to use jump leads from another taxi. So we only left at 4.40. Peter was already there. He had been at Covent Garden.
        We shan't be coming to Picketts Lock again, because it the cinema building is being demolished for a national athletics centre. But we probably wouldn't have come again anyway, because the bus service is so bad + with only 2 boys we'll stay in a hotel.
        I now have a portable CD player which makes driving along very pleasant.
